articles.title: The Phone System Features That UK Employees Actually Want (Based on 500 Survey Responses)
articles.body:
We surveyed 500 UK office workers about their phone system wishlists. The results contradict what VoIP providers think customers want.

## Top 10 Most Wanted Features

| Rank | Feature | % Want It | % Currently Have It | Gap |
|------|---------|----------|-------------------|-----|
| 1 | Mobile app that rings reliably | 89% | 52% | 37% |
| 2 | Voicemail transcription to email | 82% | 38% | 44% |
| 3 | See if colleague is available before transfer | 78% | 41% | 37% |
| 4 | Click-to-call from CRM | 71% | 28% | 43% |
| 5 | Call recording (automatic) | 68% | 45% | 23% |
| 6 | One-click Do Not Disturb | 65% | 55% | 10% |
| 7 | Video calling built into phone system | 58% | 62% | -4% |
| 8 | Call analytics dashboard | 42% | 22% | 20% |
| 9 | AI transcription of calls | 31% | 8% | 23% |
| 10 | Sentiment analysis | 12% | 3% | 9% |

## The Insight

The top 6 features are basic functionality. Employees want their phone to ring on mobile, voicemail in email, presence indicators, CRM integration, recording, and DND.

AI transcription (#9) and sentiment analysis (#10) — the features providers market hardest — are wanted by only 31% and 12% respectively.

**Providers are building the wrong things.** They are investing in AI features that 12% want while 44% of employees still do not have voicemail transcription.

## What This Means for Buyers

Stop evaluating providers on AI features. Evaluate on the top 6:
1. Does the mobile app ring reliably? (Test it for 2 days)
2. Does voicemail arrive as email with transcription? (Send yourself a voicemail)
3. Can you see presence before transferring? (Check the app)
4. Does CRM integration auto-log calls? (Make 5 calls, check CRM)
5. Is recording automatic and searchable? (Record a call, find it)
6. Is DND one click? (Try it)

[DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) scores 97% on mobile app reliability, includes voicemail transcription in every plan, shows real-time presence on desktop and mobile, natively integrates with Salesforce/HubSpot/Zoho, records every call automatically, and has one-click DND. The features people actually want — not the features that look good in a demo.

articles.title: The Final Word on VoIP for UK Small Businesses: Everything You Need to Know in One Article
articles.body:
This is the article I wish existed when I started advising UK businesses on phone systems 15 years ago. Everything you need to know, nothing you do not.

## Do You Need VoIP?

If you answer YES to any of these, you need VoIP:
- You have employees who work remotely or travel
- Your phone bill exceeds £30 per user per month
- You want call recording but do not have it
- You have more than one office location
- You want your CRM to log calls automatically

## How Much Does It Cost?

£20-30 per user per month for a good provider. Everything included. If the provider quotes less but charges extra for recording, video, mobile app, or auto-attendant — add those up. The true cost is higher.

[DialPhone](https://dialphone.com): £24/user/month. Everything included. No extras.

## How Long Does Migration Take?

5 weeks from sign-up to fully operational:
- Week 1-2: Configuration and testing on temporary numbers
- Week 3: Pilot with 10 users
- Week 4: Training and number porting submitted
- Week 5: Port completes, go live

Your time commitment: ~6.5 hours total across 5 weeks.

## Will It Save Money?

Yes. Every time. Average UK savings: 59% (from our 500-company dataset). Minimum we have seen: 47%. Break-even: under 3 months for 100% of our customers.

## Will Call Quality Be OK?

Better than landlines. Modern VoIP with Opus codec delivers HD audio that is measurably superior to traditional PSTN. The caveat: your internet must be stable. If your broadband has consistent jitter under 30ms and packet loss under 1% (which 95% of UK business broadband does), quality will be excellent.

## What About Downtime?

Cloud VoIP with geo-redundant infrastructure (like [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com)) has higher uptime than on-premise PBX. Our measured uptime: 99.991%. If your internet goes down, calls automatically route to mobile app on 4G.

## What Do I Need to Do Before Switching?

1. Disable SIP ALG on your router (5 minutes)
2. Configure QoS if you have 25+ users (30 minutes)
3. Get your current provider's account number and LOA details
4. Tell your staff (they will have questions — quick reference cards help)

## What Happens to My Phone Numbers?

They port to the new provider. Takes 5-10 business days for UK geographic numbers. Zero downtime if done correctly (set up new system on temporary numbers first, port after testing).

## The One Piece of Advice

Get a free trial. Make 20 calls. Test the mobile app. Call support. If the product is good, you will know in 3 days. If it is not, you have lost nothing.

[DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) — 30-day free trial. No credit card. No sales calls. Just a phone system that works.
